req.title: Manager creates department
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-005 mandates that authenticated managers create departments via UI form with mandatory field validation, audit logging, and specific HTTP response codes. The test suite covers: (1) the happy path with valid mandatory fields returning 200/201 and audit trail; (2) validation failures on missing/malformed mandatory fields returning 400; (3) authentication/authorization failures returning 401/403; (4) boundary conditions on field length and special characters. Heuristics: EP partitions valid/invalid/missing input states; BVA tests field length limits; NEG exercises error pathways; DT ensures decision logic coverage for role-based access.

tests.name: test_manager_creates_department_success
preconditions: session token is valid and unexpired
actions: Retrieve audit log for department creation event
expected: Audit record exists with event_type=DEPARTMENT_CREATED and actor=manager_id
Department persisted in database with correct attributes
trace: R-WEB-005: Expected outcome – department is created; audit record written; response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_department_success(auth_headers, base_url, db_connection):
    payload = {
        "name": "Engineering",
        "code": "ENG",
        "budget": 100000
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    data = resp.json()
    assert "id" in data
    assert data["name"] == "Engineering"
    audit = db_connection.query(
        "SELECT * FROM audit_log WHERE entity_id = %s",
        (data["id"],)
    )
    assert len(audit) > 0
    assert audit[0]["event_type"] == "DEPARTMENT_CREATED"

tests.name: test_manager_creates_department_missing_mandatory_field
actions: Attempt creation with null name field
expected: Response contains validation error message identifying missing field 'name'
No department record created in database
No audit record written for failed creation
trace: R-WEB-005: Error pathways – 400 on validation error
R-WEB-005: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_department_missing_mandatory_field(auth_headers, base_url, db_connection):
    payload = {"code": "ENG"}
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    error = resp.json()
    assert "name" in error.get("errors", [])
    count = db_connection.query(
        "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM departments WHERE code = %s",
        ("ENG",)
    )[0][0]
    assert count == 0

tests.name: test_manager_creates_department_malformed_budget_field
expected: Negative budget: HTTP 400 with validation error 'budget must be non-negative'
Zero budget: HTTP 200/201 or HTTP 400 depending on business rule
No department created for invalid budget value
trace: R-WEB-005: Mandatory fields must be validated
BVA: testing lower boundary and out-of-range numeric values
tests.executable:
def test_manager_creates_department_malformed_budget_field(auth_headers, base_url):
    payload_negative = {
        "name": "Sales",
        "code": "SAL",
        "budget": -5000
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_negative,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    error = resp.json()
    assert any("budget" in e.lower() for e in str(error).lower().split())
    payload_zero = {
        "name": "HR",
        "code": "HR",
        "budget": 0
    }
    resp2 = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_zero,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp2.status_code in [200, 201, 400]

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_create_department
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no valid session token)
actions: POST /departments with valid payload but no auth headers

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_create_department
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=employee (not manager)
actions: POST /departments with valid payload as employee user
trace: R-WEB-005: Error pathways – 403 if role forbidden
DT: role-based access control decision logic

tests.name: test_department_name_boundary_length
actions: POST /departments with name exceeding limit (256+ chars): {"name": "<256_char_string>", "code": "TOO", "budget": 10000}
expected: Exceeds limit (256+): HTTP 400 with error 'name exceeds maximum length'
Department created only for valid length
trace: R-WEB-005: Mandatory fields must be validated
BVA: testing upper boundary of string field length
tests.executable:
def test_department_name_boundary_length(auth_headers, base_url):
    max_name = "A" * 255
    payload_valid = {
        "name": max_name,
        "code": "LNG",
        "budget": 10000
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_valid,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    over_name = "A" * 256
    payload_invalid = {
        "name": over_name,
        "code": "TOO",
        "budget": 10000
    }
    resp2 = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_invalid,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 400

tests.name: test_department_code_special_characters_validation
actions: POST /departments with invalid characters in code: {"name": "Bad", "code": "BAD@#$", "budget": 75000}
expected: Valid alphanumeric code: HTTP 200/201, department created
Code with special chars: HTTP 400 with validation error on code field
No department created for invalid code
trace: R-WEB-005: Mandatory fields must be validated
BVA: testing boundary conditions on character set validation
tests.executable:
def test_department_code_special_characters_validation(auth_headers, base_url):
    payload_valid = {
        "name": "Operations",
        "code": "OPS123",
        "budget": 75000
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_valid,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    payload_invalid = {
        "name": "Bad",
        "code": "BAD@#$",
        "budget": 75000
    }
    resp2 = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/departments",
        json=payload_invalid,
        headers=auth_headers
    )
    assert resp2.status_code == 400
    error = resp2.json()
    assert "code" in str(error).lower()
